description: Playback Drive is a node that will take a SyncPlayback and allow you to start or stop driving it to a set of provided playback settings and values. The driving of the SyncPlayback is non networked.

fields.StartDrive: Starts driving the Target (SyncPlayback) in a non networked fashion, which essentially makes the provided Target (SyncPlayback) no longer synced until StopDrive (Call) is called.
fields.StopDrive: Stop driving the Target (SyncPlayback), making it networked again.
fields.ForceResync: When this is impulsed, The node will try to sync the current Target (SyncPlayback) position that the local user sees with everyone else in a session. (See Impulses for what a local user on an Impulse means) If this is called and NormalizedPosition (float) does not have an input, it will do nothing.
fields.Target: The Network Synced Playable that will become un-networked via being driven by this node.
fields.NormalizedPosition: The position to drive Target (SyncPlayback) to from 0 to 1.
fields.Maximum Position Error ([[Type:Float|flo: The the max amount the position on Target (SyncPlayback) can vary from NormalizedPosition (float).
fields.Speed: The a multiplier for the playback speed of Target (SyncPlayback).
fields.Play: If the Target (SyncPlayback) should be playing. (default true if normalized position is given and moving)
fields.Loop: If the Target (SyncPlayback) should loop. May clash when providing NormalizedPosition (float)
fields.OnStartDrive: When StartDrive (Call) is impulsed and it can drive Target (SyncPlayback)
fields.OnStopDrive: When StopDrive (Call) is impulsed and this node was driving Target (SyncPlayback)
fields.OnResync: sends an impulse when ForceResync (Call) succeeds.
fields.IsDriving: If the Target (SyncPlayback) is currently driving. (Aka StartDrive (Call) was called but not StopDrive (Call) yet.)
